reply:
[[BLOCK:slide27_shape1]]
Versatile  
It integrates the penalties of the LASSO (Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator) and Ridge approaches (weighted average of L1 + L2).  
Why not leverage the strengths of both methods?  
It is applicable to ultra-high dimensional data (p > n).  
This results in a slight increase in bias but a significant reduction in variance.
